source_jobs.title: Deployment Robotics Engineer (m/f/d)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Sereact is a rapidly growing embodied AI company revolutionizing industrial intelligence. We develop advanced robotics and artificial intelligence solutions that enable robotic systems to sense, reason, and act in real-world warehouse and factory environments — helping businesses streamline operations, increase productivity, and transform physical work.


ROLE OVERVIEW

As a Robotics Deployment Engineer, you will lead the installation and deployment of our AI-powered robotic systems at customer sites across Europe. This is a dynamic, hands-on engineering role that combines technical leadership with practical implementation to ensure successful system integration in high-automation production environments.

You will plan and coordinate deployments, collaborate closely with internal engineering teams, project managers and customer stakeholders, and take end-to-end responsibility for the complete installation, integration, and commissioning process onsite. This is a hands on role where you will contribute directly to mechanical and electrical work to support live systems, helping install new systems, and troubleshoot robotic systems during on-site deployments.

Your Responsibilities:


DEPLOYMENT LEADERSHIP

 - Lead the installation and commissioning of robotic systems at customer facilities.

 - Coordinate deployments with internal engineering teams, contractors, and customer personnel.

 - Plan installation workflows and ensure deployments are executed safely and efficiently.

 - Serve as the primary technical contact during on-site deployments.

 - Ensure systems meet operational, performance, and safety requirements prior to production handoff.


SYSTEM INSTALLATION & INTEGRATION

 - Install and integrate robotic arms, grippers, sensors, cameras, and mobile platforms.

 - Contribute directly to mechanical and electrical installation of robotic systems.

 - Mount and replace end-effectors such as grippers and tooling.

 - Route and secure electrical and communication cables within robotic cells and control cabinets.

 - Assist with installation, wiring, and modification of control cabinets and electrical components.

 - Drill, tap, mount, and modify brackets, fixtures, and mounting hardware as required for system integration.

 - Conduct on-site system testing and validation.

Troubleshooting & Optimization

 - Diagnose and resolve mechanical, electrical, and software issues during deployment.

 - Replace or repair hardware components such as grippers, sensors, actuators, and cabling.

 - Optimize system performance for efficiency, speed, and accuracy in production environments.

 - Provide technical training and support to customer teams.

 - Document installation procedures, configurations, and lessons learned from deployments.

 - Collaborate with internal engineering teams to improve system robustness and deployment processes.

 - Support customer sites across Europe remotely when not actively on deployment.

Travel

Willingness and ability to travel extensively across Europe (approximately 75%) is required for this role.

Qualifications:

Education and Experience

 - 3+ years of experience deploying robotic systems, automation equipment, or industrial machinery.

 - Experience leading or coordinating technical installations or field deployments.

 - Hands-on experience with robotic workcells, automation systems, or industrial equipment.

Skills

 - Knowledge of ROS 2, robotic system architecture, or industrial automation systems.

 - Proficiency in Python, C++, or similar programming languages.

 - Experience with containerization tools such as Docker is a plus.

 - Strong troubleshooting skills across mechanical, electrical, and software systems.

 - Comfortable working with hand tools, power tools, and mechanical equipment.

 - Experience installing sensors, routing cables, and integrating hardware in industrial environments.

 - Ability to read mechanical drawings, wiring diagrams, and system layouts.
